owasp_top10: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
mitre_attack: T1059.007 - Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
cwe: CWE-444 - Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests
CWE-644 - Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax
CWE-113 - Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers

threat.title: CDN Cache Poisoning via Header Manipulation
threat.description: Attackers exploit unkeyed HTTP headers (X-Forwarded-Host, X-Forwarded-Scheme, X-Original-URL) to inject malicious payloads into CDN caches. By manipulating cache keys, attackers can serve XSS payloads, phishing pages, or malicious redirects to all users requesting the poisoned resource. This vulnerability affects Cloudflare, Fastly, Akamai, and CloudFront when cache key normalization is insufficient.

versions_affected: Cloudflare (all versions without cache key normalization)
Fastly (VCL < 2.0 without custom cache key logic)

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies a web application using CloudFront CDN with default cache key settings
2. Sends crafted request with X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com header to cacheable endpoint (e.g., /static/app.js)
3. Application reflects the header value in JavaScript response without validation (e.g., var host = 'evil.com')
4. CDN caches the poisoned response using only the path as cache key (ignoring unkeyed headers)
5. Legitimate users request the same resource and receive the cached malicious JavaScript
6. XSS payload executes in victim browsers, stealing session tokens or redirecting to phishing sites
prerequisites: CDN caches responses based on incomplete cache keys (path-only, ignoring critical headers)
Application reflects or processes unkeyed headers in responses
Lack of cache key normalization for X-Forwarded-*, X-Original-URL, or custom headers
No Content-Security-Policy or insufficient CSP directives

cve_references.description: Squid proxy cache poisoning via X-Forwarded-For header allowing request smuggling
VMware vRealize Operations Manager cache poisoning via Host header manipulation
Fastly CDN cache poisoning allowing stored XSS via unkeyed headers

real_world_breaches.impact: Critical XSS on paypal.com allowing session hijacking; $18,900 bug bounty awarded
real_world_breaches.root_cause: PayPal's CDN cached responses reflecting X-Forwarded-Host header without validation

real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers cached private user data by manipulating cache keys with path traversal and header injection
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Cloudflare's default cache key excluded critical headers, allowing path-based cache poisoning

real_world_breaches.incident: Akamai CDN Header Injection via X-Original-URL
real_world_breaches.impact: Fortune 500 e-commerce site served cached XSS payloads to 50,000+ users
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Akamai Property Manager failed to include X-Original-URL in cache key, allowing header-based poisoning

real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers served malicious JavaScript to banking application users via fat GET request poisoning
real_world_breaches.root_cause: CloudFront cache behaviors did not normalize query strings or headers, allowing cache key manipulation

bug_bounty_reports.summary: PayPal cache poisoning via X-Forwarded-Host header leading to stored XSS
CDN cache poisoning on Shopify via X-Forwarded-Scheme downgrade attack
GitLab CDN cache poisoning via unkeyed headers exposing private repository content

security_research.title: Practical Web Cache Poisoning
security_research.key_finding: Identified 12,000+ vulnerable sites using unkeyed header injection; demonstrated XSS via cache poisoning on PayPal, CloudFlare, Reddit

security_research.title: Web Cache Deception Attack: A New Web Attack Vector
security_research.key_finding: Discovered technique to force CDNs to cache private user data by manipulating URL paths and cache keys

security_research.title: Bypassing Web Cache Poisoning Countermeasures
security_research.key_finding: Demonstrated advanced cache poisoning using HTTP/2 header smuggling and fat GET requests

mitigations.title: Implement CloudFront Cache Key Normalization
mitigations.description: Configure AWS CloudFront to include critical headers in cache keys and normalize query strings to prevent cache key manipulation attacks. Use Cache Policies to explicitly define which headers, cookies, and query parameters affect caching.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Create CloudFront Cache Policy with explicit header whitelist
Attach policy to distribution behavior
Test with X-Forwarded-Host header - verify cache miss on header change
Monitor CloudWatch metrics for CacheMissRate increases
Validate cache key using CloudFront-Cache-Status header

mitigations.title: Deploy Cloudflare Workers for Cache Key Sanitization
mitigations.description: Use Cloudflare Workers to intercept requests, sanitize dangerous headers, and enforce consistent cache key generation before reaching the origin server.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Deploy Worker script to Cloudflare zone
Test with curl including X-Forwarded-Host header
Verify header is stripped in origin logs
Monitor Worker analytics for errors
Validate cache hit ratio in Cloudflare Analytics

mitigations.title: Configure Varnish VCL for Cache Key Normalization
mitigations.description: Implement custom Varnish Configuration Language (VCL) rules to normalize cache keys, block dangerous headers, and prevent cache poisoning in self-hosted CDN deployments.
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
validation_steps: Deploy VCL configuration to Varnish server
Test with varnishtest: varnishtest -v cache_poisoning_test.vtc
Monitor cache hit/miss ratio: varnishstat -f MAIN.cache_hit
Verify header sanitization with varnishlog -g request -q 'ReqHeader:X-Forwarded-Host'
Load test with wrk to validate performance impact

mitigations.title: Implement Origin Server Header Validation
mitigations.description: Deploy server-side validation to reject or sanitize dangerous HTTP headers before processing requests, preventing cache poisoning even if CDN controls are bypassed.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.PT-5 - Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience
validation_steps: Install helmet and express-rate-limit: npm install helmet express-rate-limit
Add middleware to Express application
Test with curl -H 'X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com' http://localhost:3000/api/config
Verify dangerous headers are stripped in application logs
Run OWASP ZAP scan to validate header injection defenses

indicators: Sudden increase in cache MISS rate for previously cached resources
Unusual HTTP headers in CDN access logs (X-Forwarded-Host, X-Original-URL with non-standard values)
JavaScript or HTML content in cached static resources (e.g., .js, .css files)
Reports of XSS or phishing content from multiple user IPs for the same URL
Vary header mismatches between origin and CDN cached responses
Cache-Control headers being overridden or ignored
Spike in 400/403 errors after implementing header validation

log_queries.query:
// Detect cache poisoning via unkeyed header injection
AzureDiagnostics
| where Category == "FrontdoorAccessLog" or ResourceType == "FRONTDOORS"
| where requestUri_s contains "/static/" or requestUri_s endswith ".js" or requestUri_s endswith ".css"
| extend ParsedHeaders = parse_json(requestHeaders_s)
| extend XForwardedHost = tostring(ParsedHeaders["x-forwarded-host"])
| extend XOriginalUrl = tostring(ParsedHeaders["x-original-url"])
| where isnotempty(XForwardedHost) or isnotempty(XOriginalUrl)
| summarize SuspiciousRequests = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(clientIp_s) by requestUri_s, XForwardedHost, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where SuspiciousRequests > 10
| project TimeGenerated, requestUri_s, XForwardedHost, SuspiciousRequests, UniqueIPs
| order by SuspiciousRequests desc

index=cdn sourcetype=cloudfront_access_logs
| rex field=_raw "(?<x_forwarded_host>x-forwarded-host:\S+)"
| rex field=_raw "(?<x_original_url>x-original-url:\S+)"
| where isnotnull(x_forwarded_host) OR isnotnull(x_original_url)
| stats count as request_count, dc(c_ip) as unique_ips by cs_uri_stem, x_forwarded_host, x_original_url
| where request_count > 20
| eval severity = case(
    request_count > 100, "Critical",
    request_count > 50, "High",
    1=1, "Medium"
  )
| table _time, cs_uri_stem, x_forwarded_host, x_original_url, request_count, unique_ips, severity
| sort - request_count

http.request.headers.x-forwarded-host:* OR http.request.headers.x-original-url:* OR http.request.headers.x-rewrite-url:*
AND url.path:(/static/* OR *.js OR *.css)
AND NOT http.response.status_code:400
| stats count() by url.path, http.request.headers.x-forwarded-host, http.request.headers.x-original-url
| where count > 15

fields @timestamp, @message, requestParameters.headers
| filter @message like /X-Forwarded-Host|X-Original-URL|X-Rewrite-URL/
| filter requestUri like /\/static\/|\.js|\.css/
| parse @message /(?<x_forwarded_host>X-Forwarded-Host:\s*\S+)/
| parse @message /(?<x_original_url>X-Original-URL:\s*\S+)/
| stats count(*) as request_count by requestUri, x_forwarded_host, x_original_url
| filter request_count > 25
| sort request_count desc

tools.name: Burp Suite Param Miner
tools.usage: Automatically identifies cacheable endpoints vulnerable to unkeyed header injection

tools.name: CDN Analyzer
tools.usage: Validates cache key consistency across different request variations
